== Notes for [[Item:Q89390|Q89390]] ==

Aurelius Valerius Tullianus Symmachus Phosphorius (fl. 280-330) was a politician of the Roman Empire, member of the Symmachi.

He was born around 280; he had a son, Lucius Aurelius Avianius Symmachus, whose son was the famous orator Quintus Aurelius Symmachus. He was a Neoplatonist philosopher and a Pagan; it is possible that his parents were Aurelius Hermogenes, great priest of Asia, and Tullia Valeria, great priestess.

In 319 was proconsul of Achaea, in 330 became Consul.

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
66F2DEBBB91201EFBBE49822EF79916C6A17
geni:6000000010828462367

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
